samples.xml_description:
<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal traffic light operation, the east-west (EW) light should have a 60-clock cycle period, with 5 clock cycles of yellow and 10 clock cycles of red. The north-south (NS) light should have a 60-clock cycle period, with 5 clock cycles of yellow and 10 clock cycles of red. When the pedestrian button is pressed, if the remaining EW green time is greater than 10 clocks, force it to be 10 clocks. If the remaining EW green time is less than 10 clocks, do not change the remaining time. The EW and NS lights are paired. When the EW light is green, the NS light is red. When the EW light is red, the NS light is green.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Pedestrian button input.</input>
        <output id="red">Red output signal.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow output signal.</output>
        <output id="green">Green output signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum name="idle">Idle state.</enum>
        <enum name="s1_red">State 1 red.</enum>
        <enum name="s2_yellow">State 2 yellow.</enum>
        <enum name="s3_green">State 3 green.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic>
            <reg id="cnt" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit counter.</reg>
            <reg id="state" width_description="[1:0]">2-bit state.</reg>
            <reg id="p_red">1-bit pedestrian red.</reg>
            <reg id="p_yellow">1-bit pedestrian yellow.</reg>
            <reg id="p_green">1-bit pedestrian green.</reg>
            <always>
                <case>
                    <if expr="state==idle">Idle state.</if>
                    <if expr="state==s1_red">State 1 red.</if>
                    <if expr="state==s2_yellow">State 2 yellow.</if>
                    <if expr="state==s3_green">State 3 green.</if>
                </case>
            </always>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic>
            <if expr="!rst_n">Reset counter.</if>
            <else>
                <if expr="pass_request && green">Force green.</if>
                <else>
                    <if expr="!green && p_green">Force green.</if>
                    <else>
                        <if expr="!yellow && p_yellow">Force yellow.</if>
                        <else>
                            <if expr="!red && p_red">Force red.</if>
                        </else>
                    </else>
                </else>
            </else>
        </sequential_logic>
        <assign id="clock">cnt.</assign>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Implement a traffic light controller. The first always block is the state transition logic. It uses a case statement to handle different states. The second always block is the timing logic. The counter is decremented by 1 on every positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal. The counter values are adjusted based on the state. If the reset signal is low, the counter is set to 10. If the pass_request signal is active and the green signal is active, the counter is set to 10. If the green signal is inactive and the previous green signal (p_green) was active, the counter is set to 60. If the yellow signal is inactive and the previous yellow signal (p_yellow) was active, the counter is set to 5. If the red signal is inactive and the previous red signal (p_red) was active, the counter is set to 10. Otherwise, the counter is decremented normally. The assign statement assigns the value of the internal counter (cnt) to the output clock. The final always block is the output logic. It assigns the previous values (p_red, p_yellow, p_green) to the output signals (red, yellow, green) on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal.</implementation>
</module>
